symptomSummary: After a prompt revision accumulates ~30+ invocations, the evolve command ignores recent failures and regresses, optimizing against stale older feedback instead.
rootCauseHypothesis: The `forRevision` repository method fetches invocations with `orderBy(asc(date))` and a hard `limit(25)`. Once a revision has more than 25 invocations, only the oldest 25 are returned — recent flagged outputs are silently dropped from the evolve context.
fixHypothesis: Change the `orderBy` in `forRevision` from `asc(schema.invocations.date)` to `desc(schema.invocations.date)` so the query returns the N most-recent invocations instead of the N oldest, matching the already-correct `forArtifact` method which already uses `desc` order.

primaryLocation.rationale: Line 120 uses `orderBy(asc(schema.invocations.date))` with `.limit(limit)` where limit defaults to 25. SQL ASC+LIMIT returns the earliest rows first — the oldest 25 invocations ever recorded for that revision. The evolve path in src/index.ts:201 calls `store.invocationsForRevision(active.id, telemetryWindow)` where telemetryWindow defaults to 25, so once invocation count exceeds 25 the feedback window is permanently anchored to the oldest records and never advances. The ~30-invocation symptom threshold is exactly consistent with a LIMIT-25 ascending query.

candidateLocations.rationale: The evolve function passes `telemetryWindow` (default 25) to the repository without any recency guard. Even after fixing the sort order, this is a good place to add defense-in-depth recency filtering or to document the ordering contract expected of store adapters.
The store interface declares `invocationsForRevision` without specifying sort semantics. Any alternative adapter could independently implement ascending-first ordering and exhibit the same bug.
The KV store is a parallel implementation of the same interface. If it iterates invocations without recency ordering it would exhibit the same regression for users on the KV backend.
The API route for evolve may pass a telemetryWindow value to the core evolve function; if it forwards hardcoded limits it could amplify or mask the underlying ordering problem.
